summary: Use generic sqlite runtime-name handlers when multiple foreign bindings share `sqlite:queryOne` or `sqlite:queryAll`; per-binding adapter installation collides on `runtimeName` and silently routes later bindings over earlier ones.
ergonomics: The existing host-binding metadata and runtime contract plumbing made it easy to add sqlite operations without touching the checker or emitter.

The current runtime installation path is biased toward unique runtime names, which becomes a trap for capability-style namespaces like `sqlite:*` where many typed bindings intentionally share one operation name.
The compiled end-to-end test harness in `test/Main.hs` is effective for storage/runtime slices because it exercises actual emitted modules and the Bun runtime together.
follow_ups: If sqlite needs scalar queries, add explicit runtime names such as `sqlite:queryValue` / `sqlite:queryValues` or extend the contract so installed runtime functions can recover the calling binding identity.
Consider extracting the sqlite runtime-name installation pattern into a shared helper so other namespace-style runtimes do not repeat the same duplicate-`runtimeName` failure mode.
The generated JS validator still appears sensitive to record fields literally named `value`; this task worked around it in the regression, but the emitter bug is worth a separate fix.
warnings: Do not install sqlite bindings through `__claspAdaptHostBindings` when several foreign declarations reuse the same `sqlite:*` runtime name; only the last adapted binding survives under that key.

When writing sqlite runtime regressions, prefer compiled wrapper declarations (`firstNote`, `noteRows`, etc.) over calling foreign declarations directly from JS, because foreign declarations themselves are not exported callable surfaces.
Avoid using a record field named `value` in new emitted-JS runtime tests until the validator naming collision is fixed.
residual_risks: SQLite query installation now installs generic runtime-name handlers instead of per-binding adapters so duplicate `sqlite:*` runtime names can coexist; that keeps compiled calls correct, but direct host-side use of installed sqlite bindings no longer gets the extra per-binding adapter round-trip.

The new slice covers record-shaped row mapping for `sqlite:queryOne` and `sqlite:queryAll`; primitive single-column query returns were intentionally left out to avoid ambiguous behavior across bindings that share the same runtime name.
